signals.question: Will the next Prime Minister of Hungary be Péter Magyar?
evidence: 21 Research Institute poll (March 23-28): Tisza 56% vs Fidesz 37% among decided voters — 19pp lead
Medián Institute poll: Tisza 58% vs Fidesz 35% among likely voters — 23pp margin, potentially sufficient for two-thirds supermajority
Seat projections: Tisza 129/199 seats vs Fidesz 64 seats — stable parliamentary majority
Bloomberg (April 1): 'Hungarian opposition poll surge shows Orban faces election loss'
Euronews (April 1): Tisza 'cements lead ahead of April elections'
signals.reasoning: Hungarian parliamentary election is April 12 (10 days away). Every major poll shows Tisza winning by 19-23pp among decided voters, with seat projections giving Magyar a clear majority (129/199). Even with Hungary's ±3pp polling error and urban-rural distribution concerns, a 19-23pp lead is essentially insurmountable. Market at 66.5% significantly underprices the ~82% probability of Magyar becoming PM.

signals.question: Erdoğan out by December 31, 2026?
evidence: Erdogan's presidential term ends in 2028 — no constitutional mechanism for removal before then without impeachment or resignation
Despite mass protests after Imamoglu arrest (March 2025), Erdogan remains firmly in power one year later (Al-Monitor, March 2026)
Time Magazine analysis: 'Why Turkey's Pro-Democracy Protests Probably Won't Succeed' — authoritarian consolidation limits protest effectiveness
Brookings analysis: Erdogan controls military, judiciary, and media apparatus; opposition leader Imamoglu remains jailed
Foreign Affairs: 'The End of Erdogan' notes his power ends constitutionally in 2028, not before; early elections require his consent
signals.reasoning: Erdogan's term runs to 2028 and he controls all levers of power. Despite unprecedented protests after Imamoglu's arrest, historical base rate for authoritarian leaders being forced out before their term ends is very low (~5-7%). Opposition calls for elections 'by November' but Erdogan decides when elections happen. Market at 16.5% overprices the probability of removal by >9pp.

signals.question: Will Trump be impeached by end of 2026?
evidence: Republicans control the House 220-215 in the 119th Congress (through Jan 3, 2027) — impeachment requires simple House majority
CNN (Jan 2026): Democratic leadership under Jeffries is 'steering caucus away from bombastic moves against the president' to focus on midterm messaging
Even if Democrats win the November 2026 midterms, the 120th Congress is not sworn in until January 3, 2027 — after the Dec 31 deadline
Congress.gov: H.Res.353 and H.Res.939 (impeachment resolutions) have been introduced but have no Republican co-sponsors and no committee action
Newsweek: impeachment odds briefly hit 24% in November 2025 over Epstein files but returned to baseline as no Republican defections materialized
signals.reasoning: Republicans control the House through January 3, 2027. Impeachment before Dec 31, 2026 requires Republicans to impeach their own president — this has never happened and there are zero Republican co-sponsors on existing resolutions. Even a Democratic midterm wave in November 2026 doesn't change the math since new members aren't seated until January 2027. Market at 12% overprices a ~3% event.
